ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general latte beverage entry; no caramel-flavoured latte specific code exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKM covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no caramel latte-specific code exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for caramel latte beverage. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard medium-sized caramel latte served in a café cup of approximately 250 ml, equivalent to roughly 258 g, is the typical single-serve portion consumed in Singapore coffee shops and international café chains.

nutrient_source.title: Caramel Latte — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all per_100g fields. Values are accepted as sourced from the HPB Singapore Foodo database. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for caffe latte with whole milk and caramel syrup (USDA SR Legacy item 14209 / FDC ID 171890) as a secondary cross-reference. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and water are set to -1 as HPB does not report these for this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for caramel latte in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 62 (medium) based on the closest analogous entry: sweetened café latte with whole milk and sugar syrup. The GI of milk-based coffee beverages with added sugar is driven primarily by the lactose and sucrose content; GI ~55–65 is consistent with published values for sweetened milk-coffee drinks. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (62 × 11.2) / 100 = 6.9 per 258 g serving (4.35 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2.58 = 11.2 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Added caramel syrup (added sugar, low fibre, potassium 152 mg/100 g below the 200 mg offset threshold) triggers the sweetened flavoured beverage concern for hypertension management.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should request sugar-free caramel syrup or switch to a plain unsweetened latte, as the added sugar and low potassium content of the standard caramel latte do not support blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened flavoured beverage with added caramel syrup; classified as an SSB regardless of the absolute sugar figure (4.1 g/100 g), triggering the SSB hard gate for Type 2 Diabetes management.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid standard caramel latte due to its added caramel syrup; a plain unsweetened latte or black coffee with no added sugar is the recommended alternative to avoid postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~0.97 g/100 g from whole milk) and added sugar from caramel syrup contribute to cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly; cholesterol content is low at 7 mg/100 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, regular consumption of caramel latte should be limited due to its added sugar and saturated fat content; switching to a skim-milk unsweetened latte reduces both saturated fat and added sugar load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added caramel syrup provides refined sugar with no anti-inflammatory value; the beverage lacks positive anti-inflammatory factors.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request sugar-free syrup or switch to a plain latte, as added sugar may promote systemic inflammation; plain espresso provides chlorogenic acid with potential anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Caramel Latte is a popular espresso-based coffee beverage made by combining a shot or double shot of espresso with steamed whole milk and caramel-flavoured syrup, typically finished with a light milk foam. The drink is widely available in international café chains and local Singaporean coffee shops. It has a smooth, creamy texture with a sweet caramel flavour overlaying the mild bitterness of espresso. Per 100 g, it provides 43 kcal, 2.81 g protein, 1.61 g fat, and 4.35 g carbohydrate (of which 4.06 g is sugar), with 35 mg sodium, 152 mg potassium, and 105 mg calcium. The beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened flavoured beverage (SSB) due to the addition of caramel syrup, which makes it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension without modification. The milk component contributes meaningful calcium (105 mg/100 g) and protein (2.81 g/100 g). Clinically, the added sugar content and SSB classification are the primary concerns; plain unsweetened latte or black coffee are recommended alternatives for health-conscious consumers. The glycemic index is estimated at 62 (medium) based on analogous sweetened milk-coffee beverages, with a glycemic load of approximately 6.9 per standard 250 ml serving.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a caramel latte?
Is caramel latte suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of caramel latte?
Is caramel latte high in caffeine?
Can I drink caramel latte if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between a latte and a caramel latte?
How much protein is in a caramel latte?

confidence_reason: All core per_100g nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. The food is a well-defined café beverage with a canonical composition. Saturated fat is cross-referenced from USDA FoodData Central for the closest analogous entry. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, which is the only approved GI source.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB for this beverage; this is a confirmed zero per the HPB source, consistent with a liquid milk-coffee beverage containing no fibre-bearing solid ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and moisture data for this specific caramel latte entry are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported for this entry in the HPB database. Saturated fat estimated at 0.97 g/100 g from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171890 (caffe latte, whole milk) as a secondary cross-reference since HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. The health_suitability assessments from the input record have been preserved and mapped into health_context with the addition of cardiovascular disease as required by the Veritas standard.
